# UC-13.7.12 · Fluentd Hot-Reload Configuration Change Governance and Drift Detection

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Change, Configuration &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We watch for moments when logging agents reload their settings and compare those moments to the approved automation trail. We catch rapid flip-flopping reloads or edits that do not match the team playbook. We help you show whether a sudden burst of errors started right after someone pushed a new config.*

---

## Description

Detects Fluentd configuration change governance failures and risky reload anti-patterns by correlating supervisor hot-reload and restart signals, Kubernetes ConfigMap audit mutations, ingest metadata that carries config_hash or git commit identifiers, downstream error or drop spike windows, and Splunk CIM Change activity on collector hosts—distinct from UC-13.7.1 through UC-13.7.11 narratives that center throughput, worker health, buffers, Fluent Bit latency, filter drops, filesystem backlog, TLS faults, multiline stuck states, or tag routing taxonomy alone. The analytic foregrounds who reloaded, when, with which fingerprint, whether the reload succeeded, and whether manual or automated change velocity fights declared GitOps cadence.

## Value

Platform governance and audit stakeholders receive a time ordered, multi-source narrative that ties logging pipeline instability to configuration change events, which shortens rollback debates, proves drift from approved automation, and preserves evidence for regulated change attestations without conflating the story with generic buffer health metrics.

## Implementation

Land fluentd:log reload and downstream slices on index=fluent, kube:audit ConfigMap verbs on index=kubernetes, governance httpevent metadata with hashes on index=fluent, and accelerated CIM Change summaries for collector hosts. Maintain fluentd_change_owners.csv with cadence and ownership. Schedule observability_uc_13_7_12_fluentd_hot_reload_governance every fifteen minutes using earliest=-24h@h. Tune severity thresholds after two sprint cycles, throttle duplicate merge_key pages for thirty minutes, and route critical alerts to platform logging on-call with Git diff links.

## Evidence

Saved search observability_uc_13_7_12_fluentd_hot_reload_governance with fifteen minute schedule; dashboard drilldowns on multisearch lanes; weekly fluentd_change_owners.csv export archived to a restricted evidence index.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

In a lab logging namespace, introduce a deliberate Fluentd syntax error, observe Fluent::ConfigError in fluentd:log, confirm kube:audit shows a ConfigMap patch, metadata events show a new config_hash, observability_uc_13_7_12_fluentd_hot_reload_governance yields critical within thirty minutes, then restore clean YAML and verify severity returns to low.

### Negative scenario

After a governed GitOps merge, reload counts stay within fluentd_change_owners.csv cadence, metadata hash matches the merge commit, audit shows only the automation account, and downstream error lanes remain flat for thirty consecutive scheduled runs with thresholds calibrated to fleet noise.

## Detailed Implementation

### Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with the observability pipeline team that operates Fluentd under td-agent or fluent-package on Linux, Kubernetes platform engineers who mount ConfigMaps and sidecars such as configmap-reload, GitOps operators who reconcile Argo CD or Flux repositories, and Splunk administrators who index agent logs, audit streams, and HTTP event collector metadata events. This use case isolates configuration change governance for Fluentd hot reload paths: POSIX signal driven reloads including SIGHUP, supervisor mediated restarts when processes run under the under-supervisor flag, round trips triggered after ConfigMap volume refreshes, and failure text such as Fluent::ConfigError or missing file errors that prove a reload attempt did not land cleanly. The angle is deliberately not UC-13.7.1 raw input or output throughput collapse, not UC-13.7.2 retry storms, not UC-13.7.3 disk or network input output saturation, not UC-13.7.4 worker crash loops, not UC-13.7.5 memory buffer pressure, not UC-13.7.6 Fluent Bit flush latency service levels, not UC-13.7.7 filter drop percentages in isolation, not UC-13.7.8 filesystem storage backlog, not UC-13.7.9 Transport Layer Security handshake failures, not UC-13.7.10 multiline parser stuck states, and not UC-13.7.11 tag routing taxonomy drift. Here the failure mode is change velocity and provenance: operators or controllers that reload too often, edits that never match Git, syntax errors that force rollback, or reload bursts that precede downstream error spikes and drop spikes so teams can prove a change preceded instability.

Understanding Fluentd lifecycle signals requires reading supervisor logs alongside cluster audit evidence. A benign reload prints Reloading config file lines while a broken edit surfaces ConfigError class text. Manual SSH edits may issue SIGHUP outside GitOps, producing fingerprints that disagree with merge commits recorded in webhook fed lookups. ConfigMap reloader sidecars can thrash when controllers rewrite annotations every minute even when semantic config is unchanged, so rate and coalesce signals matter as much as presence. Splunk HTTP event collector channels that emit config_hash or git_commit fields after render steps give a third witness independent of node local logs. Kubernetes audit verbs on configmaps document who changed data plane YAML even when pods already recycled.

Risk briefing: legitimate blue green promotions may intentionally reload many agents within minutes; expected_reload_cadence_min columns in fluentd_change_owners.csv must reflect those windows. Canary clusters with short lived namespaces create noisy fingerprints until baselines refresh. Legal reviewers should confirm whether config_fingerprint values embed customer identifiers before exposing them in shared dashboards. Break glass roles may need wider access to fluent indexes than general analysts receive.

RBAC and safety: jobs that export audit streams need read only cluster roles. Service accounts that call Git APIs for commit metadata should use least privilege tokens rotated on schedule. Change calendars should stamp ticket identifiers onto related events so Splunk timelines align with approved rollouts.

Differentiation recap: reload and restart events, supervisor versus hot reload semantics, ConfigMap audit correlation, HTTP event collector emitted config hashes, downstream error burst correlation, Splunk Common Information Model Change summaries for collector hosts, anti pattern thrash scoring, and ownership overlays from fluentd_change_owners.csv, not buffer byte rates alone, not tag pattern mismatches as the primary story, not multiline health.

### Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up five multisearch arms Splunk can fuse without assuming a single telemetry lane always works during incidents.

Fluentd supervisor log arm: forward Fluentd own logs into index=fluent with sourcetype=fluentd:log. Capture host, cluster, kubernetes namespace annotations when collectors run in Kubernetes, and raw lines that include Reloading config file, signal handling text, under-supervisor restart banners, and ConfigError class messages. Normalize UTC timestamps and strip ANSI color. Map kubernetes.pod_name into host when bare host is missing. Preserve optional structured fields config_version or checksum when your chart emits them.

Kubernetes audit arm: land kube:audit events into index=kubernetes with objectRef.resource=configmaps when your control plane enables audit logging for config changes. Retain user.username, objectRef.namespace, requestObject.metadata.resourceVersion, and verbs such as update or patch. Align cluster labels with the same strings used in Fluentd logs so joins do not fracture during multi cluster fleets.

Splunk HTTP event collector metadata arm: emit lightweight governance events on index=fluent with sourcetype=httpevent whenever a render pipeline produces a new fluentd_config_event that carries config_hash, config_version, or git_commit. These events may originate from CI jobs or admission hooks; they should never include secrets. Use separate tokens per environment.

Downstream instability arm: keep a parallel slice of fluentd:log lines that show emit errors, flush failures, pattern surprises after reload, or connection refused text, while excluding the narrow Reloading config file phrase so the arm emphasizes post change instability rather than the change banner itself.

Splunk Common Information Model Change arm: accelerate Change summaries for hosts and objects tied to logging configuration. The search uses tstats against datamodel=Change with nodename=Change so configuration edits on collector nodes correlate with reload bursts; post filter in dashboards for objects whose names reference fluentd or kubernetes when noise requires narrowing.

Maintain fluentd_change_owners.csv with columns cluster, host, owner_team, expected_reload_cadence_min, gitops_tool, and approved_change_channel. Refresh weekly from configuration management databases or automated inventory jobs. Sister datacenter pairs should carry matching rows to avoid false drift when one region legitimately omits a workload class.

Example operator helpers for design reviews:

kubectl -n logging get configmap fluentd-config -o yaml and compare resourceVersion timestamps to kube audit rows.
kill -HUP of a test td-agent in lab only with change approval, then confirm Reloading lines in Splunk before expanding to production.
curl Git provider webhooks or pipeline APIs for commit sha tied to the same second as HTTP event collector metadata events.

props.conf guidance: TRUNCATE must accommodate long config_fingerprint strings. LINE_BREAKER should keep stack traces intact when ConfigError raises ruby backtraces. FIELDALIAS maps should unify dotted versus flattened kubernetes labels.

Pre-save validation: each arm must return events in lab within one hour of enabling collection. Induce a harmless syntax error in non production, confirm ConfigError text appears, audit rows show ConfigMap patch, HTTP event collector metadata shows a new hash, downstream arm captures at least one controlled error line after rollback, and tstats returns Change rows after a deliberate host level edit.

### Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as observability_uc_13_7_12_fluentd_hot_reload_governance with a fifteen minute schedule during steady-state production, dispatch earliest=-24h@h, dispatch latest=now, and alert when severity is critical or high after tuning for fleet noise. Throttle duplicate cluster, host, merge_key tuples for thirty minutes unless severity escalates. Include recommended_action tokens in pager bodies so incident commanders open runbooks without rerunning ad hoc searches.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans Fluentd supervisor reload lines, Kubernetes ConfigMap audit patches, Splunk HTTP event collector metadata hashes, downstream instability lines, and Change datamodel summaries so a silent single lane cannot hide a governance crisis. coalesce ladders absorb field naming drift across exporters and kubernetes labels. streamstats builds rolling sums of reload and error bursts per merge key. eventstats adds per cluster medians for downstream error lanes and sums ConfigMap updates per namespace window. case maps combined signals into critical, high, medium, and low tiers. inputlookup joins enrich rows with owner_team, expected_reload_cadence_min, gitops_tool, and approved_change_channel for accountability overlays.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.7.12 Fluentd hot-reload config governance SIGHUP supervisor ConfigMap audit HEC config_hash downstream error correlation; earliest=-24h@h")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=fluent sourcetype=fluentd:log earliest=-24h@h latest=now
        ("Reloading config file" OR "Reloading" OR "restarting" OR "SIGHUP" OR "SIGUSR1" OR "SIGUSR2" OR "Received signal" OR "under-supervisor" OR "error_class=Fluent::ConfigError" OR "Fluent::ConfigError" OR "Cannot find a config file")
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,k8s_cluster_name,kubernetes_cluster,eks_cluster,"unknown_cluster"))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,kubernetes_pod_name,splunk_server,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval k8s_ns=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(kubernetes_namespace,k8s_namespace,namespace,""))))
      | rex field=_raw max_match=1 "(?i)(config_hash|config_version|checksum|git_sha)[=:\"\s]+(?<cfgtok>[A-Za-z0-9._:+/-]{6,128})"
      | eval config_fingerprint=trim(toString(coalesce(config_hash,config_version,checksum,git_commit,cfgtok,"reload_log_line")))
      | eval reload_event=if(match(_raw,"(?i)Reloading|restarting|SIGHUP|SIGUSR|Received signal|under-supervisor"),1,0)
      | eval config_error=if(match(_raw,"(?i)ConfigError|Cannot find a config"),1,0)
      | eval cm_update_evt=0
      | eval hec_hash_evt=0
      | eval downstream_err_evt=if(config_error==1,1,0)
      | eval chg_activity=0
      | eval lane="fluentd_reload_supervisor_log"
      | fields _time cluster host k8s_ns lane config_fingerprint reload_event config_error cm_update_evt hec_hash_evt downstream_err_evt chg_activity ]
    [ search index=kubernetes sourcetype=kube:audit earliest=-24h@h latest=now objectRef.resource=configmaps (verb=update OR verb=patch)
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,k8s_cluster_name,eks_cluster,"unknown_cluster"))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(user.username,objectRef.name,"k8s_audit_actor"))))
      | eval k8s_ns=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(objectRef.namespace,kubernetes_namespace,namespace,""))))
      | eval config_fingerprint=trim(toString(coalesce(requestObject.metadata.resourceVersion,objectRef.uid,"configmap_audit")))
      | eval reload_event=0
      | eval config_error=0
      | eval cm_update_evt=1
      | eval hec_hash_evt=0
      | eval downstream_err_evt=0
      | eval chg_activity=0
      | eval lane="kubernetes_audit_configmap"
      | fields _time cluster host k8s_ns lane config_fingerprint reload_event config_error cm_update_evt hec_hash_evt downstream_err_evt chg_activity ]
    [ search index=fluent sourcetype=httpevent earliest=-24h@h latest=now (config_hash=* OR config_version=* OR git_commit=* OR fluentd_config_event=*)
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,k8s_cluster_name,dc,"unknown_cluster"))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,splunk_server,source,"hec_emitter"))))
      | eval k8s_ns=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(namespace,k8s_ns,""))))
      | eval config_fingerprint=trim(toString(coalesce(config_hash,config_version,git_commit,checksum,"hec_metadata")))
      | eval reload_event=0
      | eval config_error=0
      | eval cm_update_evt=0
      | eval hec_hash_evt=1
      | eval downstream_err_evt=0
      | eval chg_activity=0
      | eval lane="splunk_hec_config_metadata"
      | fields _time cluster host k8s_ns lane config_fingerprint reload_event config_error cm_update_evt hec_hash_evt downstream_err_evt chg_activity ]
    [ search index=fluent sourcetype=fluentd:log earliest=-24h@h latest=now ("emit error" OR "BufferOverflowOutputError" OR "pattern not matched" OR "failed to flush" OR "connection refused")
        NOT "Reloading config file"
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,k8s_cluster_name,kubernetes_cluster,"unknown_cluster"))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,kubernetes_pod_name,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval k8s_ns=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(kubernetes_namespace,k8s_namespace,namespace,""))))
      | eval config_fingerprint="downstream_instability_lane"
      | eval reload_event=0
      | eval config_error=0
      | eval cm_update_evt=0
      | eval hec_hash_evt=0
      | eval downstream_err_evt=1
      | eval chg_activity=0
      | eval lane="fluentd_downstream_error_lane"
      | fields _time cluster host k8s_ns lane config_fingerprint reload_event config_error cm_update_evt hec_hash_evt downstream_err_evt chg_activity ]
    [ | tstats summariesonly=f latest(_time) AS _time count AS chg_cnt latest(Change.object) AS chg_object latest(Change.user) AS change_user FROM datamodel=Change WHERE nodename=Change earliest=-7d@d latest=now BY Change.dest
      | rename Change.dest AS host
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval cluster="cim_change_correlation_lane"
      | eval k8s_ns=""
      | eval config_fingerprint=trim(toString(coalesce(chg_object,"change_datamodel")))
      | eval reload_event=0
      | eval config_error=0
      | eval cm_update_evt=0
      | eval hec_hash_evt=0
      | eval downstream_err_evt=0
      | eval chg_activity=chg_cnt
      | eval lane="cim_change_tstats_lane"
      | fields _time cluster host k8s_ns lane config_fingerprint reload_event config_error cm_update_evt hec_hash_evt downstream_err_evt chg_activity change_user ]
| eval merge_key=strcat(cluster,"|",host,"|",coalesce(k8s_ns,"root_ns"))
| streamstats window=18 global=f sum(reload_event) AS reload_burst BY merge_key
| streamstats window=18 global=f sum(downstream_err_evt) AS err_burst BY merge_key
| eventstats median(downstream_err_evt) AS err_lane_p50 stdev(downstream_err_evt) AS err_lane_sd BY cluster lane
| eventstats sum(cm_update_evt) AS cm_updates_window BY cluster k8s_ns
| join type=left max=0 cluster host
    [| inputlookup fluentd_change_owners.csv
     | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(cluster)))
     | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
     | fields cluster host owner_team expected_reload_cadence_min gitops_tool approved_change_channel ]
| eval cadence_floor=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(expected_reload_cadence_min,"30")),10)
| eval anti_pattern=if(reload_burst>0 AND cadence_floor>0 AND reload_burst>(1440/max(cadence_floor,1))*2,1,0)
| eval severity=case(config_error==1 AND reload_event>=1,"critical", reload_burst>=25 OR anti_pattern==1,"high", err_burst>=12 AND reload_burst>=3,"high", cm_updates_window>=8 AND reload_burst>=6,"medium", err_lane_sd>0 AND downstream_err_evt>coalesce(err_lane_p50,0)*3,"medium", true(),"low")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="critical","rollback_config_fix_syntax_and_resubmit_gitops_merge", severity=="high","stop_thrashing_reloads_reconcile_gitops_with_manual_ssh", severity=="medium","correlate_configmap_audit_to_hec_hash_and_change_dm", true(),"refresh_fluentd_change_owners_lookup_and_baselines")
| table _time cluster host k8s_ns lane config_fingerprint reload_burst err_burst cm_updates_window severity recommended_action owner_team gitops_tool anti_pattern merge_key chg_activity```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -24h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch observability_uc_13_7_12_fluentd_hot_reload_governance | where severity IN ("critical","high")

Performance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch imbalance, materialize each arm hourly into a summary index keyed on merge_key, widen alert searches on summaries, and retain this full search for investigations. Cap wildcard volume on httpevent with production index allowlists after security agrees you are not dropping rare evidence rows.

Dashboard design: timeline of reload_burst and err_burst, matrix of cluster by config_fingerprint, drilldowns to kube audit JSON, httpevent metadata samples, fluentd:log excerpts, and Change object text for the same host.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins on the suspect collector. Compare live process start time and last SIGHUP handling notes to the Splunk row for the same host inside the last thirty minutes. When timestamps disagree, verify stale pod labels after restarts and duplicate DaemonSets before blaming GitOps.

Cross-check configuration: print active file path from the live process, then diff Git for the same path hash. When Git shows a fix but Splunk still shows old fingerprints, suspect incomplete reload or two ConfigMaps mounted to different containers.

Downstream validation: pick a high severity row, open downstream error lane samples in Splunk, and confirm timestamps cluster immediately after reload_burst spikes. Pair with sister datacenter traffic: when only one region spikes, diff fluentd_change_owners.csv rows and chart versions.

Synthetic validation: in a lab namespace, inject a deliberate syntax error, confirm observability_uc_13_7_12_fluentd_hot_reload_governance returns critical within two evaluation windows, restore clean config, reload, and confirm severity returns to low.

Negative test: on a healthy cluster after merges settle, filter severity to critical and high and expect zero rows for thirty consecutive runs when thresholds match your calibrated noise floor.

Runbook linkage: document expected reload bursts during single namespace canary deploys so validators do not file false defects when change calendars show an approved rollout.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — GitOps merge completes and a coordinated reload fans out: confirm HTTP event collector metadata hash matches commit, audit shows approved actor, reload_burst aligns with fleet size, and err_burst stays flat; archive the timeline as evidence of a healthy promotion.

Case 2 — Manual SSH edit triggers SIGHUP while Git still shows the prior sha: treat as drift, open approved_change_channel review, capture diff from privileged sessions, and restore Git as source of truth before closing the incident.

Case 3 — ConfigMap reloader thrashes every minute with identical semantic bytes: tune reloader flags or webhook debounce, raise expected_reload_cadence_min only after platform agrees, and verify anti_pattern scoring stabilizes.

Case 4 — Reload succeeds in logs yet Fluent::ConfigError appears in the same window: often a partial file write or race; investigate atomic write wrappers, volume mount delays, and supervisor ordering.

Case 5 — Supervisor restart path versus hot reload: compare under-supervisor lines to plain worker recycle events; some plugins require full restart and will never hot swap cleanly.

Case 6 — Reload during heavy flush: correlate buffer queue metrics in separate dashboards, pause aggressive reload scripts during backlog, and schedule maintenance windows for schema heavy edits.

Case 7 — Reload during Transport Layer Security handshake churn: pair with UC-13.7.9 playbooks but keep this card focused on proving the reload event preceded the handshake spike.

Case 8 — Schema change forces worker recycle: document when match blocks require process exit, update runbooks to expect restart counts not only SIGHUP counts, and align pager text accordingly.

Case 9 — Dual active high availability pair reloads out of phase: compare config_fingerprint per node, ensure load balancer health checks tolerate staggered reloads, and avoid split brain outputs.

Case 10 — Hot reload silently comments out a match stanza: diff rendered files, compare HTTP event collector arrival lanes before and after, and restore stanza under change control.

Case 11 — kubectl rollout status or Deployment events show recycle without matching Git sha: chase orphaned ReplicaSets, stale Helm secrets, or manual kubectl apply outside the pipeline.

Case 12 — Inventory lookup stale expected_reload_cadence_min causes chronic medium severity: refresh cadence from real promotion schedules, document seasonal events, and automate replication from pipeline calendars.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists five arms including fluent sourcetype=fluentd:log reload lane, kubernetes sourcetype=kube:audit configmaps, fluent sourcetype=httpevent metadata, fluent sourcetype=fluentd:log downstream instability, and tstats Change; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, inputlookup, and join appear; final table lists fourteen analyst-facing columns including recommended_action and merge_key.

Extended platform narrative for fleet operators: treat fluentd_change_owners.csv rows as first-class configuration items alongside network policies. When platform engineers promote a new logging chart, require a pull request that updates expected_reload_cadence_min using real pipeline calendars to avoid alert thrash. Document ordering rules for reload scripts in the same repository that stores Helm values so interactive SIGHUP cannot bypass review gates. For hybrid estates, annotate approved_change_channel with whether Argo CD or Flux owns the reconcile loop to prevent duplicate automation fighting the same ConfigMap.

Version governance: maintain rolling minimum chart versions per cluster and fail change pipelines when a region drifts behind a reference region without a recorded exception. Pair release note review with this control so supervisor behavior changes are not lost in routine image bumps. When vendor distributions lag upstream Fluentd, annotate lookup notes with distribution-specific identifiers to prevent false confidence during audits.

Downstream Splunk parsing: intermediate forwarders must not strip config_fingerprint fields that investigations rely on. When this search shows healthy reload counts yet analysts cannot find metadata hashes, move investigation to CI systems and mirror evidence in the configuration management database. Keep httpevent field extractions consistent across clusters so the metadata arm does not silently empty during schema migrations.

Governance overlays: stamp change_ticket_id on configuration snapshot events when Helm values change. Archive weekly snapshots of fluentd_change_owners.csv to a restricted evidence index for auditors who require ownership traceability. Pair alert outputs with pipeline_region labels so large enterprises route pages to regional squads without manual lookup.

Advanced forensics: when reload_burst oscillates with batch schedules, separate service identities for cron driven namespaces using role labels in fluentd_change_owners.csv rather than muting the entire cluster. For ephemeral pods that appear briefly, ensure inventory jobs run frequently enough to capture host identities before baselines misclassify them as long term drift.

Capacity footnote: this control intentionally does not optimize search cost. If multisearch runtime exceeds service level objectives, pre-aggregate arms into fifteen minute summary indexes keyed on cluster, host, and config_fingerprint while retaining raw arms for investigations. Security isolation: restrict httpevent indexes to roles that require metadata content; audit-only roles can retain kubernetes and internal Change arms for operational dashboards without reading application bodies.

Collaboration with developers: require service owners to publish expected reload windows whenever they change logging drivers or admission hooks. Insert those windows into runbooks linked from fluentd_change_owners.csv so SRE teams regression test timing assumptions rather than relying on memory.

Closing operational guidance: rehearse this saved search quarterly with tabletop exercises that include observability, Kubernetes platform, and GitOps leads. Rotate runbook URLs when teams reorganize so pager deep links do not rot. Keep a single source of truth for sister datacenter pairing labels to prevent mistaken cross region comparisons during partial failovers.

Platform hygiene addendum: document how supervisor restarts interact with plugin lazy load behavior so junior engineers do not assume every reload is interchangeable with SIGHUP. Capture explicit screenshots of dashboards that plot reload_burst per cluster so executives receive familiar visuals during reviews. When service meshes inject sidecars that rewrite metadata, add mesh revision columns to lookup CSVs to explain sudden fingerprint bursts without invoking security incidents.

Evidence retention stance: keep ninety days of fluentd supervisor warnings in compliance with corporate log retention policy, and shorter hot retention for high volume httpevent indexes if legal approves summarization for this control. Ensure backup jobs include fluentd_change_owners.csv history so accidental spreadsheet edits can be reconstructed during audits.

Training narrative: onboarding modules should walk new engineers through a single end to end example where a manual SIGHUP demonstrably disagrees with Git sha metadata, shows audit and HTTP event collector witnesses, and restores governance after reverting the edit. Practical exercises beat abstract documentation for this failure mode.

Final assurance statement: this card is written so an engineer who has never touched Fluentd can still follow the arms, interpret severity, and open the correct Git directory within one incident rotation, provided lookup hygiene and role based access prerequisites are satisfied.

Supplemental depth for international deployments: multilingual operator notes may appear in ticket bodies while Fluentd logs remain English; normalize pager templates to English excerpts only yet preserve ticket language in drilldown links. Cross border clusters may prohibit exporting raw audit user names; hash actor identifiers in a derived field when legal mandates minimization while retaining merge keys for technical correlation. Clock skew between Git provider timestamps, Kubernetes apiservers, and Splunk indexing time can reorder apparent cause and effect; always compare _time versus indexed_time when disputes arise.

Runbook integration: link observability_uc_13_7_12_fluentd_hot_reload_governance to configuration management tickets automatically using lookup approved_change_channel URLs. When channels rot, fail CI linting on stale hyperlinks so documentation debt surfaces early. For regulated industries, attach hash algorithms used to compute config_fingerprint fields so auditors understand collision risk.

Future instrumentation: optional OpenTelemetry logs or events from GitOps controllers can augment multisearch when kube audit retention is short; treat those feeds as additive, not replacements, because audit remains the authoritative control plane record. Windows hosted Fluentd builds are out of primary scope yet may appear in hybrid fleets; extend host normalization cautiously and test field extractions on Windows event text before production rollout.

Quality gate: reviewers should confirm no single arm dominates severity every week; if Change alone triggers medium constantly, narrow object filters rather than disabling the arm, so loss of Fluentd logs during an outage still preserves partial signal.


## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-13.7.12 Fluentd hot-reload config governance SIGHUP supervisor ConfigMap audit HEC config_hash downstream error correlation; earliest=-24h@h")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=fluent sourcetype=fluentd:log earliest=-24h@h latest=now
        ("Reloading config file" OR "Reloading" OR "restarting" OR "SIGHUP" OR "SIGUSR1" OR "SIGUSR2" OR "Received signal" OR "under-supervisor" OR "error_class=Fluent::ConfigError" OR "Fluent::ConfigError" OR "Cannot find a config file")
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,k8s_cluster_name,kubernetes_cluster,eks_cluster,"unknown_cluster"))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,kubernetes_pod_name,splunk_server,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval k8s_ns=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(kubernetes_namespace,k8s_namespace,namespace,""))))
      | rex field=_raw max_match=1 "(?i)(config_hash|config_version|checksum|git_sha)[=:\"\s]+(?<cfgtok>[A-Za-z0-9._:+/-]{6,128})"
      | eval config_fingerprint=trim(toString(coalesce(config_hash,config_version,checksum,git_commit,cfgtok,"reload_log_line")))
      | eval reload_event=if(match(_raw,"(?i)Reloading|restarting|SIGHUP|SIGUSR|Received signal|under-supervisor"),1,0)
      | eval config_error=if(match(_raw,"(?i)ConfigError|Cannot find a config"),1,0)
      | eval cm_update_evt=0
      | eval hec_hash_evt=0
      | eval downstream_err_evt=if(config_error==1,1,0)
      | eval chg_activity=0
      | eval lane="fluentd_reload_supervisor_log"
      | fields _time cluster host k8s_ns lane config_fingerprint reload_event config_error cm_update_evt hec_hash_evt downstream_err_evt chg_activity ]
    [ search index=kubernetes sourcetype=kube:audit earliest=-24h@h latest=now objectRef.resource=configmaps (verb=update OR verb=patch)
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,k8s_cluster_name,eks_cluster,"unknown_cluster"))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(user.username,objectRef.name,"k8s_audit_actor"))))
      | eval k8s_ns=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(objectRef.namespace,kubernetes_namespace,namespace,""))))
      | eval config_fingerprint=trim(toString(coalesce(requestObject.metadata.resourceVersion,objectRef.uid,"configmap_audit")))
      | eval reload_event=0
      | eval config_error=0
      | eval cm_update_evt=1
      | eval hec_hash_evt=0
      | eval downstream_err_evt=0
      | eval chg_activity=0
      | eval lane="kubernetes_audit_configmap"
      | fields _time cluster host k8s_ns lane config_fingerprint reload_event config_error cm_update_evt hec_hash_evt downstream_err_evt chg_activity ]
    [ search index=fluent sourcetype=httpevent earliest=-24h@h latest=now (config_hash=* OR config_version=* OR git_commit=* OR fluentd_config_event=*)
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,k8s_cluster_name,dc,"unknown_cluster"))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,splunk_server,source,"hec_emitter"))))
      | eval k8s_ns=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(namespace,k8s_ns,""))))
      | eval config_fingerprint=trim(toString(coalesce(config_hash,config_version,git_commit,checksum,"hec_metadata")))
      | eval reload_event=0
      | eval config_error=0
      | eval cm_update_evt=0
      | eval hec_hash_evt=1
      | eval downstream_err_evt=0
      | eval chg_activity=0
      | eval lane="splunk_hec_config_metadata"
      | fields _time cluster host k8s_ns lane config_fingerprint reload_event config_error cm_update_evt hec_hash_evt downstream_err_evt chg_activity ]
    [ search index=fluent sourcetype=fluentd:log earliest=-24h@h latest=now ("emit error" OR "BufferOverflowOutputError" OR "pattern not matched" OR "failed to flush" OR "connection refused")
        NOT "Reloading config file"
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,k8s_cluster_name,kubernetes_cluster,"unknown_cluster"))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,kubernetes_pod_name,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval k8s_ns=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(kubernetes_namespace,k8s_namespace,namespace,""))))
      | eval config_fingerprint="downstream_instability_lane"
      | eval reload_event=0
      | eval config_error=0
      | eval cm_update_evt=0
      | eval hec_hash_evt=0
      | eval downstream_err_evt=1
      | eval chg_activity=0
      | eval lane="fluentd_downstream_error_lane"
      | fields _time cluster host k8s_ns lane config_fingerprint reload_event config_error cm_update_evt hec_hash_evt downstream_err_evt chg_activity ]
    [ | tstats summariesonly=f latest(_time) AS _time count AS chg_cnt latest(Change.object) AS chg_object latest(Change.user) AS change_user FROM datamodel=Change WHERE nodename=Change earliest=-7d@d latest=now BY Change.dest
      | rename Change.dest AS host
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval cluster="cim_change_correlation_lane"
      | eval k8s_ns=""
      | eval config_fingerprint=trim(toString(coalesce(chg_object,"change_datamodel")))
      | eval reload_event=0
      | eval config_error=0
      | eval cm_update_evt=0
      | eval hec_hash_evt=0
      | eval downstream_err_evt=0
      | eval chg_activity=chg_cnt
      | eval lane="cim_change_tstats_lane"
      | fields _time cluster host k8s_ns lane config_fingerprint reload_event config_error cm_update_evt hec_hash_evt downstream_err_evt chg_activity change_user ]
| eval merge_key=cluster."|".host."|".coalesce(k8s_ns,"root_ns")
| streamstats window=18 global=f sum(reload_event) AS reload_burst BY merge_key
| streamstats window=18 global=f sum(downstream_err_evt) AS err_burst BY merge_key
| eventstats median(downstream_err_evt) AS err_lane_p50 stdev(downstream_err_evt) AS err_lane_sd BY cluster lane
| eventstats sum(cm_update_evt) AS cm_updates_window BY cluster k8s_ns
| join type=left max=0 cluster host
    [| inputlookup fluentd_change_owners.csv
     | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(cluster)))
     | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
     | fields cluster host owner_team expected_reload_cadence_min gitops_tool approved_change_channel ]
| eval cadence_floor=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(expected_reload_cadence_min,"30")),10)
| eval anti_pattern=if(reload_burst>0 AND cadence_floor>0 AND reload_burst>(1440/max(cadence_floor,1))*2,1,0)
| eval severity=case(config_error==1 AND reload_event>=1,"critical", reload_burst>=25 OR anti_pattern==1,"high", err_burst>=12 AND reload_burst>=3,"high", cm_updates_window>=8 AND reload_burst>=6,"medium", err_lane_sd>0 AND downstream_err_evt>coalesce(err_lane_p50,0)*3,"medium", true(),"low")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="critical","rollback_config_fix_syntax_and_resubmit_gitops_merge", severity=="high","stop_thrashing_reloads_reconcile_gitops_with_manual_ssh", severity=="medium","correlate_configmap_audit_to_hec_hash_and_change_dm", true(),"refresh_fluentd_change_owners_lookup_and_baselines")
| table _time cluster host k8s_ns lane config_fingerprint reload_burst err_burst cm_updates_window severity recommended_action owner_team gitops_tool anti_pattern merge_key chg_activity
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Change.action) AS chg_action latest(Change.object) AS chg_object count FROM datamodel=Change WHERE nodename=Change earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Change.dest Change.user
| rename Change.dest AS chg_host
| eval chg_host=lower(trim(toString(chg_host)))
| table chg_host Change.user chg_action chg_object count
```

## Visualization

Timeline of reload_burst and err_burst with vertical markers for ConfigMap audit timestamps, sortable table of cluster by config_fingerprint and owner_team, single-value ratio of anti_pattern reloads, and overlay of downstream error rate following each reload window.

## Known False Positives

Blue green promotions legitimately raise reload_burst across large fleets until fluentd_change_owners.csv cadence reflects the window; correlate approved_change_channel tickets before paging. Kubernetes audit host fields often carry apiserver user names rather than pod hostnames, so join enrichment to owner_team may miss until you normalize actor_to_collector_host.csv style mappings. HTTP event collector metadata can lag Git merges when pipelines batch renders; compare indexed_time slack. ConfigMap controllers that bump resourceVersion without semantic edits still increment cm_updates_window; pair with diff tools outside Splunk when noise persists. Downstream error lanes include flush failures unrelated to reload when destinations saturate; validate destination health before blaming config churn. Change datamodel arms include unrelated host activity; narrow Change.object filters in dashboards. Canary clusters with ephemeral pods inflate merge_key cardinality; scope alerts to production labels. Reloader sidecars in crash loops emit restart storms that resemble anti_pattern scoring; inspect sidecar logs directly. Seasonal traffic drops shrink error baselines and can flatten err_burst comparisons when applications genuinely idled.
